prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. The Royal Opera House in London was the venue for the 67th British Academy Film Awards, also known as the BAFTAs, on 16 February 2014. The awards were awarded for the best national and foreign films of 2013. On 8 January 2014, actor Luke Evans and actress Helen McCrory announced their nominations. The award was presented by the British Academy of Film and Television Arts, and Gravity won six of its eleven nominations.
B. The 67th iteration of the British Academy Film Awards, frequently referred to as the BAFTAs, took place on February 16, 2014, at London's Royal Opera House. This event recognized the most outstanding cinematic achievements, both domestic and international, from the year 2013. The list of nominees was unveiled on January 8, 2014, by the performers Luke Evans and Helen McCrory. The British Academy of Film and Television Arts organized the ceremony, distributing awards for the top feature films and documentaries, regardless of their country of origin, that were exhibited in British movie theaters during 2013. *Gravity* secured victory in six categories out of its eleven nominations, which included the award for Best Director for Alfonso Cuarón, Outstanding British Film, Best Cinematography, Best Original Music, Best Sound, and Best Special Visual Effects. *12 Years a Slave* earned the awards for Best Film and Best Actor in a Leading Role, presented to Chiwetel Ejiofor. Cate Blanchett also received recognition.
C. The 67th British Academy Film Awards, more commonly known as the BAFTAs, were held on 16 February 2014 at the Royal Opera House in London, honouring the best national and foreign films of 2013. The nominations were announced on 8 January 2014 by actor Luke Evans and actress Helen McCrory. Presented by the British Academy of Film and Television Arts, accolades were handed out for the best feature-length film and documentaries of any nationality that were screened at British cinemas in 2013.Gravity won six of its eleven nominations, including Best Director for Alfonso Cuarón, Outstanding British Film, Best Cinematography, Best Original Music, Best Sound, and Best Special Visual Effects. 12 Years a Slave won Best Film and Best Actor in a Leading Role for Chiwetel Ejiofor. Cate Blanchett

Answer:

Cate Blanchett also received recognition.
